pixelFontResults.note: Block PixelFont/AntiAliasedPixelFont glyphs; same glyphs reached exact match in M283-M285 when rendered at correct aspect into 48x320, so font fidelity is not the dominant residual cause.

antiAliasedPixelFontResults.note: Used for centered fixtures in BuildSyntheticFullImageFixture; not the dominant residual cause vs geometry.

directCropResults.explanation: crop rendered directly at correct aspect ratio into the 48x320 recognizer canvas

detectorCropResults.explanation: axis-aligned crop stretched to fixed 48x320 with per-axis nearest neighbour; aspect ratio destroyed

auditFindings: ROOT CAUSE: BuildRecognizerTensorFromImageCrop stretched crops to fixed [48,320] (scaleX=w/320, scaleY=h/48, nearest neighbour), distorting aspect ratio.
Isolation evidence: identical block glyphs decoded EXACTLY in M283-M285 at correct aspect, so the variable is geometry/resize, not synthetic font fidelity.
Error signatures (V->Y, M->IA, RO->E, char/space loss) are consistent with non-uniform horizontal stretch and nearest-neighbour aliasing.
PaddleOCR resize_norm_img preserves aspect: height 48, width=min(320, ceil(48*aspect)), bilinear, (x-0.5)/0.5, right-pad with 0.0.
Two inconsistent recognizer preprocessors existed: M198 (height 32, ImageNet mean/std) vs M283 pipeline (height 48, stretch). Neither matched PaddleOCR fully.
Resampling was nearest-neighbour instead of bilinear (cv2.resize default).
Crop bridge is axis-aligned; PaddleOCR/RapidOCR use get_rotate_crop_image (4-point perspective) - low impact for horizontal synthetic text but a real divergence.
implementedFixes: Added NodalOsPaddleOcrRecognizerCropPreprocessor (PaddleOCR resize_norm_img: aspect-preserving bilinear resize + right-pad 0.0 + (x-0.5)/0.5).
Wired BuildRecognizerTensorFromImageCrop to delegate to the aligned preprocessor; default RatioPreservingRightPad; --recognizer-resize stretch retained for A/B.
Added 7 deterministic ONNX-free unit tests proving geometry, padding, normalization and stretch-vs-ratio divergence.
recommendedNextStep: Run --synthetic-detector-to-recognizer-pipeline-probe A/B (--recognizer-resize ratio vs stretch) out-of-process and capture per-fixture decode/edit-distance; if aspect-preserving reaches exact/normalized on >=3 fixtures, advance to READY_FOR_INTERNAL_CONTROLLED_REAL_IMAGE_FIXTURES.
fullSuiteNote: OCR filters 152 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed. Full suite failures are pre-existing flaky browser-runtime smoke tests (e.g. BrowserLauncherUsesProfileSessionManagerForDefaultDisposableProfile - temp profile directory cleanup race); 2 of 3 passed on isolated rerun. Unrelated to OCR.
